class.name: InputHapticEffect
class.brief_description: Abstract base class for input haptic (force feedback) effects.
class.description: Abstract base class of all types of input haptic (force feedback) effects.

members.member: Direction of the effect expressed in cartesian coordinates. Cartesian coordinates are similar to 3D vectors. If you draw a straight line on graph paper with an origin of [code](0, 0)[/code] at the center of the page, the direction of the line can be defined by the coordinates of any intersection that it crosses, regardless of the distance from the origin. A direction of [code](1, -2)[/code] and a direction of [code](5, -10)[/code] are exactly the same. [b]Note:[/b] The coordinates used in creating force-feedback effects define only direction, not magnitude or distance. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee417536(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.
Duration of the effect, in seconds.

constants.constant: A haptic effect that represents a constant force. See also [InputHapticEffectConstant]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416339(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a periodic force with sine waveform. See also [InputHapticEffectPeriodic]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418719(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a periodic force with square waveform. See also [InputHapticEffectPeriodic]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418719(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a periodic force with triangle waveform. See also [InputHapticEffectPeriodic]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418719(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a periodic force with sawtooth up waveform (the waveform drops vertically after it reaches maximum positive force). See also [InputHapticEffectPeriodic]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418719(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a periodic force with sawtooth down waveform (the waveform rises vertically after it reaches maximum negative force). See also [InputHapticEffectPeriodic]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418719(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a ramp force. See also [InputHapticEffectRamp]. A ramp force is a force with defined starting and ending magnitudes and a finite duration. A ramp force can continue in a single direction, or it can start as a strong push in one direction, weaken, stop, and then strengthen in the opposite direction. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee418773(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a conditional force with spring condition effect (the force increases in proportion to the distance of the axis from a defined neutral point). See also [InputHapticEffectCondition]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a conditional force with damper condition effect (the force increases in proportion to the velocity with which the user moves the axis). See also [InputHapticEffectCondition]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a conditional force with inertia condition effect (the force increases in proportion to the acceleration of the axis). See also [InputHapticEffectCondition]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.

A haptic effect that represents a conditional force with friction condition effect (the force is applied when the axis is moved and depends on the defined friction coefficient). See also [InputHapticEffectCondition]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416335(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.
A haptic effect for direct control over high/low frequency motors. See also [InputHapticEffectLeftRight].

A haptic effect that represents a custom force. See also [InputHapticEffectCustom]. [b]Note:[/b] See [url=https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/desktop/ee416371(v=vs.85)]DirectInput documentation[/url] for more information.
